返回本批次

Rockstar Games 遭黑客攻击，黑客威胁称如果不支付赎金将导致大规模数据泄露

Rockstar Games Hacked, Hackers Threaten a Massive Data Leak If Not Paid Ransom

kotaku.com 作者 c420 分数 100 评论 50 2026/04/12 00:04
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 对于 Rockstar Games 来说还有更多坏消息。这一次，老牌黑客组织 ShinyHunters 声称已经突破了该公司的安全云服务器，并声称掌握了大量数据。该组织希望在 4 月 14 日之前支付数字赎金，否则据报道该组织将泄露其所掌握的信息。
  2. 更新：美国东部时间 4 月 11 日上午 11:45：Rockstar Games 确认发生了数据泄露事件。一位发言人向 Kotaku 发送了这份声明：“我们可以确认，有限数量的非重大公司信息因第三方数据泄露而被访问。这一事件对我们的组织或我们的玩家没有影响。”原故事继续如下……据 Cyber​​sec Guru 和 Hackread 报道，4 月 11 日，该黑客组织在其专门泄密的暗网网站上发布消息称，该组织已获得对 Rockstar Snowflake 服务器的访问权限，该服务器是一家与许多大公司合作的云托管公司和提供商。以下是该组织在周六分享的消息：“Rockstar Games，您的 Snowflake 实例因 Anodot.com 受到损害。要么付费，要么泄露。这是我们在泄露之前发出的最终警告，并会在 2026 年 4 月 14 日之前与您联系，同时还会出现一些烦人的（数字）问题。做出正确的决定，不要成为下一个头条新闻。”然而，黑客实际上并没有破解 Snowflake 的安全性。相反，据报道，该组织通过 Anodot 获得了数据访问权限，Anodot 是一种云成本监控和分析软件服务，Rockstar 和其他公司使用该服务来管理云数据。最近有报道称，Anodot 遭遇了安全漏洞，这可能为 ShinyHunters 提供了访问安全 Snowflake 数据的途径。据称，该组织使用的方法在 Rockstar 看来是合法的，这意味着该组织现在可能拥有大量数据。
  3. ShinyHunters 尚未公开说明他们有权访问并可能泄露哪些数据或文件，但据信他们无权访问密码或个人玩家数据。相反，这次黑客攻击的重点是公司信息和资产，其中可能包括合同、财务文件、营销计划以及 Rockstar Games 几天内可能不希望在网上公开共享的其他数据。
  4. ShinyHunters 自 2020 年以来一直存在，通常追踪大公司。过去的目标包括 Microsoft、Ticketmaster、Cisco、AT&T 和 Wattpad。他们试图勒索数据或出售数据。如果该组织声称入侵了 Rockstar 并窃取了数据，那么他们很可能并不是在虚张声势。 2022 年，Rockstar Games 遭受了一次臭名昭著的黑客攻击，导致大量早期 GTA 6 游戏片段和资产在网上泄露。这次黑客攻击是由一名青少年实施的，他能够访问该公司的 Slack 聊天服务。这名英国青少年后来被判处终身监禁，在医院监狱服刑，只有在医生认为他不再对他人构成危险的情况下才会被释放。

Original Summary

  1. More bad news for Rockstar Games. This time, established hacker group ShinyHunters is claiming to have breached the company’s secured cloud servers and now claims to have their hands on a large collection of data. The group wants a digital ransom to be paid by April 14, or it will reportedly leak what it has.
  2. Update: 4/11/26, 11:45 a.m. ET: Rockstar Games confirmed that a data breach has happened. A spokesperson sent over this statement to Kotaku: “We can confirm that a limited amount of non-material company information was accessed in connection with a third-party data breach. This incident has no impact on our organization or our players.” Original story continues below… As reported by Cybersec Guru and Hackread, on April 11, the hacker group posted on its dark web site dedicated to leaks that it had gained access to Rockstar’s Snowflake servers, a cloud-hosting company and provider that works with many large companies. Here is the message the group shared on Saturday: “Rockstar Games, your Snowflake instances were compromised thanks to Anodot.com. Pay or leak. This is a final warning to reach out by 14 Apr 2026 before we leak, along with several annoying (digital) problems that’ll come your way. Make the right decision, don’t be the next headline.” The hackers didn’t actually crack Snowflake’s security, however. Instead, it’s reported that the group gained access to the data via Anodot, a cloud cost monitoring and analytics software service that Rockstar and other companies use to manage cloud data. It has recently been reported that Anodot has suffered a security breach, possibly providing a path for ShinyHunters to gain access to secure Snowflake data. The method the group used allegedly would have looked legit to Rockstar, meaning the group likely has a lot of data on its hands now.
  3. ShinyHunters hasn’t publicly stated what data or files they have access to and could leak, though it’s believed they don’t have access to passwords or personal player data. Instead, this hack was focused on corporate information and assets, which could include contracts, financial documents, marketing plans, and other data that Rockstar Games would likely not want publicly shared online in a few days.
  4. The ShinyHunters have been around since 2020 and typically go after large companies. Past targets have included Microsoft, Ticketmaster, Cisco, AT&T, and Wattpad. They try to ransom data or sell it. If the group is claiming to have breached Rockstar and stolen data, they likely aren’t bluffing. In 2022, Rockstar Games suffered an infamous hack that led to a lot of early GTA 6 gameplay footage and assets being leaked online. That hack was pulled off by a teenager who was able to gain access to the company’s Slack chat service. The UK teen was later sentenced to life in a hospital prison and will only be released in the future if doctors decide he’s no longer a danger to others.

评论（默认中文）

joshmn 2026/04/12 04:33
我和 Sebastien Raoult 一起在联邦监狱里，他是 ShinyHunters 的成员之一。我们在同一个单位，经常聊天。

当我的投标进行到一半时，另一名囚犯告诉我“B3 的新人是另一位黑客”。我真的很兴奋——至少我能有人和我谈论工作。

我从他那里得到的收获是，他们是一群当代的“脚本小子”，他们有很多空闲时间。

这跟踪。

I was in federal prison with Sebastien Raoult, one of the ShinyHunters guys. We were in the same unit and talked regularly.

I was about mid-way through my bid when another inmate told me "new guy in B3 is a another hacker." I got really excited—I'd have someone to talk shop with, at the very least.

My takeaway from him was that they're a bunch of contemporary "script kiddies" with a lot of time on their hands.

This tracks.

cbg0 2026/04/12 01:16
更新：美国东部时间 4 月 11 日上午 11:45：Rockstar Games 确认发生了数据泄露事件。一位发言人向 Kotaku 转交了这份声明：

“我们可以确认，有限数量的非重大公司信息因第三方数据泄露而被访问。这一事件对我们的组织或我们的玩家没有影响。”

Update: 4/11/26, 11:45 a.m. ET: Rockstar Games confirmed that a data breach has happened. A spokesperson sent over this statement to Kotaku:

“We can confirm that a limited amount of non-material company information was accessed in connection with a third-party data breach. This incident has no impact on our organization or our players.”

plmpsu 2026/04/12 02:03

That's what I would say regardless of if I was considering paying or not.
在 HN 中查看
embedding-shape 2026/04/12 01:33
> “Rockstar Games，您的 Snowflake 实例因 Anodot.com 受到损害。要么付费，要么泄漏。这是我们在泄漏之前于 2026 年 4 月 14 日之前发出的最后警告，同时还会出现一些烦人的（数字）问题。做出正确的决定，不要成为下一个头条新闻。”

任何熟悉“雪花”的人都可以说出通常在那里托管什么类型的数据吗？从网站和缺乏数据细节来看，我猜它与资产、文物和类似的东西有关，而更多地与财务数据和一般/通用“业务”东西有关？

> “Rockstar Games, your Snowflake instances were compromised thanks to Anodot.com. Pay or leak. This is a final warning to reach out by 14 Apr 2026 before we leak, along with several annoying (digital) problems that’ll come your way. Make the right decision, don’t be the next headline.”

Anyone familiar with "Snowflake" enough to say what sort of data was typically hosted there? Judging by the website and the lack of specifics about the data, I'm guessing it's less about assets, artifacts and stuff like that, and more about financial data and general/generic "business" stuff?

在 HN 中查看
Maxion 2026/04/12 01:44
以我的经验来看，Snowflake经常被用作数据仓库。
In my experienced Snowflake is often used as a data warehouse.
在 HN 中查看
hilariously 2026/04/12 01:42
它是一个数据库，但您基本上可以在那里存储任何 OLAP 类型的内容，例如用于聚合客户数据的所有内容。
It's a database, but you could store basically any OLAP type things there, all your stuff for aggregate customer data for instance.
在 HN 中查看
embedding-shape 2026/04/12 01:43
当然可以，但有些数据库的销售/购买更多是通过品牌知名度和数据类型而不是实际的技术能力。不要问我为什么，只是非常熟悉人们做出这样的选择。
Sure, but some databases are sold/bought more by brand recognition and for the type of data rather than actual technical capabilities. Don't ask me why, just very familiar with people making those sort of choices.
在 HN 中查看
tempaccount5050 2026/04/12 02:27
我见过一些公司确实将他们拥有的每一条数据镜像到雪花中来进行人工智能/分析工作。里面大概有很多屎吧。
I've seen companies literally mirror every piece of data they have in snowflake to do AI/analytics stuff. There's probably a lot of of shit in there.
在 HN 中查看
ziml77 2026/04/12 03:13
根据我的经验，雪花通常用于数据分析。它很可能包含财务内容，但不像原始文件。绝对不是源代码。

我的意思是，从技术上讲，您可以将文档填充到具有 BINARY 数据类型的列中，前提是每个文档小于 67 MB，但它并不是真正用作文档存储。

Snowflake is typically used for data analytics in my experience. It's going to have financial stuff very likely, but not like raw documents. Definitely not source code.

I mean technically you can stuff documents into a column with the BINARY datatype provided they are under 67 MB each, but it's not really meant to be used as a document store.

在 HN 中查看
Bender 2026/04/12 03:49
Rockstar Games 遭黑客攻击，黑客威胁称如果不支付赎金将导致大规模数据泄露

这只是我的观点，但这并不是太大的威胁，我认为他们应该忽略它。 Rockstars 的社交平台一直存在令人厌恶的安全性，玩家总是能够轻松地互相欺骗，知道其他玩家住在哪里，将彼此从游戏中引导到需要多个模组菜单才能进入多人游戏大厅的程度，根据我的经验，因此从雪花中勒索玩家数据的金钱是多余的。

Rockstar Games Hacked, Hackers Threaten a Massive Data Leak If Not Paid Ransom

This is just my opinion but that is not much of a threat and I think they should ignore it. Rockstars social platform has always had abhorrent security and players have always been able to easily doxx one another, know where other players live, boot each other out of games to the point of requiring multiple mod-menus just to be in a multi-player lobby in my experience thus extortion of money for player data from snowflake is just redundant.

> players have always been able to easily doxx one another, know where other players live,

That sounds unlikely.

rundigen12 2026/04/12 01:03
老实说，我预计需求是“尽快发布 GTA 6，否则我们就会发布”。 ......他们只是要钱这一事实有点令人失望。 ;-)
I honestly expected the demand to be "Release GTA 6 soon or else we will". ...The fact that they're just demanding money is a little disappointing. ;-)
在 HN 中查看
gloxkiqcza 2026/04/12 01:37
如今，许多勒索软件团体的运作方式与合法科技初创公司的运作方式相同。这是一个目标明确的大型组织，而不是一些人在胡闹。这是一个有趣的想法。
Many ransomware groups of today operate in the same way a legal tech startup would. It’s a large organization with clear goals, not just some guys fooling around. It’s a funny thought tho.
在 HN 中查看
specialist 2026/04/12 02:59
像我这样的外行（新手）如何了解这些东西？喜欢《连线》杂志的技术水平。

我刚刚开始播客《暗网日记》。太棒了。

当我处理电子病历时，我认为我们（也）被黑客攻击只是时间问题。所有最平庸的原因：许多供应商、共享密码、root/admin 访问权限等。

我想从那以后情况并没有太大改善。

How do laypersons (noobs) like me learn about this stuff? Like Wired magazine technical level.

I've just started Darknet Diaries podcast. So great.

When I worked on electronic medical records, I assumed it was just a matter of time until we were hacked (too). All the most banal reasons: many vendors, shared passwords, root/admin access, etc.

I imagine things haven't improved much since.

cyberpunk 2026/04/12 04:01

Darknet Drinking Game: a shot every time something is “unbelievable to me”

robotburrito 2026/04/12 02:09
是的，但大型科技公司的运作方式不仅仅是违反这样的法律。
Yeah but large tech companies don’t just operate by breaking laws like this.
在 HN 中查看
luqtas 2026/04/12 02:14
允许大玩家伤害少数群体的法律有什么好处？ Rockstar 最近因员工虐待和裁员而举行罢工
laws that allow big players hurt minorities are any good? Rockstar recently had a strike from their workers by their abuse and layoffs
在 HN 中查看
EA-3167 2026/04/12 02:41
当然，Uber 可能是这方面最著名的，但也有很多人破坏了东西，然后支付罚款。

事实上，我想说这种违法行为是彻头彻尾的例行公事。关键的区别在于是否有能力聘请真正优秀的法律和游说团队。

Sure they do, Uber is probably the most famous in that regard, but plenty break things and pay a fine later.

In fact I’d say that sort of law breaking is downright routine. The key difference is the ability to afford a really good legal and lobbying team.

chistev 2026/04/12 05:34

在 HN 中查看
chistev 2026/04/12 02:03
巧合且有趣的是，我再次阅读了 2015 年的一篇旧帖子，标题为 - ProtonMail 支付了 6000 美元赎金，无论如何都被 DDoS 攻击了

置顶评论说——

“永远不要支付赎金。 请。即使你的生意会受到影响，如果你付钱的话，它也会遭受更多的损失，因为现在知道你会屈服。另外：你正在让其他人面临更大的问题。”

该评论的最高回复是——

“来自他们的博客： https://protonmaildotcom.wordpress.com/ 下午 2 点左右，攻击者开始直接攻击我们上游提供商的基础设施和数据中心本身。对我们 ISP 的协同攻击超过了 100Gbps，不仅攻击了数据中心，还攻击了苏黎世、法兰克福以及我们 ISP 拥有节点的其他地点的路由器。这种对关键基础设施的协调攻击最终导致数据中心和 ISP 瘫痪，这影响了数百家其他公司，而不仅仅是 ProtonMail。

此时，我们受到第三方的巨大压力，要求我们支付赎金，我们勉强同意在日内瓦时间下午 3:30 向比特币地址 1FxHcZzW3z9NRSUnQ9Pcp58ddYaSuN1T2y 支付赎金。这是所有受影响公司做出的集体决定，虽然我们不同意，但考虑到其他公司因我们的攻击而遭受了数十万瑞士法郎的损失，我们尊重这一决定。我们希望通过付款，可以让其他公司免受针对我们的攻击的影响，但攻击仍在继续。这显然是一个错误的决定，所以让我们向所有未来的攻击者明确表示——ProtonMail 永远不会再支付赎金。 ”

完整线程在这里 -

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=10523583

Coincidentally and Interestingly, again, I was reading an old thread from 2015 titled - ProtonMail pays $6k ransom, gets taken out by DDoS anyway

The top comment says -

"NEVER EVER PAY RANSOM MONEY. Please. Even if your business will suffer it will suffer a lot more if you do pay since now it is known you'll cave. Also: you are making the problem larger for others."

The top response to that comment says -

"From their blog: https://protonmaildotcom.wordpress.com/ At around 2PM, the attackers began directly attacking the infrastructure of our upstream providers and the datacenter itself. The coordinated assault on our ISP exceeded 100Gbps and attacked not only the datacenter, but also routers in Zurich, Frankfurt, and other locations where our ISP has nodes. This coordinated assault on key infrastructure eventually managed to bring down both the datacenter and the ISP, which impacted hundreds of other companies, not just ProtonMail.

At this point, we were placed under a lot of pressure by third parties to just pay the ransom, which we grudgingly agreed to do at 3:30PM Geneva time to the bitcoin address 1FxHcZzW3z9NRSUnQ9Pcp58ddYaSuN1T2y. This was a collective decision taken by all impacted companies, and while we disagree with it, we nevertheless respected it taking into the consideration the hundreds of thousands of Swiss Francs in damages suffered by other companies caught up in the attack against us. We hoped that by paying, we could spare the other companies impacted by the attack against us, but the attacks continued nevertheless. This was clearly a wrong decision so let us be clear to all future attackers – ProtonMail will NEVER pay another ransom. "

Full thread here -

https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=10523583

在 HN 中查看
ronsor 2026/04/12 02:13
如今，大多数黑客如果支付了赎金，实际上都会信守诺言。

这听起来很反常，但激励措施要求这样做：如果付款不能带来解决方案，那么没有人会付款。结果，所有的大团伙都避免了诈骗。

Most hackers actually keep their promises if paid the ransom, nowadays.

It sounds perverse but the incentives require it: if payment didn't bring resolution, no one would pay. As a result, all of the big gangs avoid scamming.

在 HN 中查看
mh- 2026/04/12 02:26
这也是 2015 年的情况。在该组织没有提出其他要求的情况下，如果他们根本无法让它停止（在技术层面上），我不会感到惊讶。

很久以前，意外地使正在打包的节点饱和是一种很常见的错误 从。那么你的 C&C 也无法让他们做出回应。哎呀。

That was the state of play in 2015 as well. In the absence of a claim from the group otherwise, I wouldn't be surprised if they simply couldn't get it to stop (on a technical level.)

Way back when, it was a pretty common screwup to accidentally saturate the nodes you were packeting from. So then your C&C couldn't get them to respond, either. Oops.

在 HN 中查看
xoa 2026/04/12 04:04
>如今，大多数黑客如果支付了赎金，实际上都会信守诺言。

我认为这实际上不是真的，或者至少不能被视为理所当然。相反，赎金似乎更多地遵循了硅谷风险投资的道路：

。这听起来很反常，但激励措施要求这样做：如果付款不能带来解决方案，那么没有人会付款。结果，所有的大团伙都避免了诈骗。

您所描述的是长期的预期博弈论结果 迭代的 游戏。只要支付金额在潜在支付范围的 <salary> 一侧，这种方法就有效，其中每笔支付对于勒索者投入的工作来说很可能是体面的钱，但又不会太多以至于他们不需要新的赎金。如果/当支付的绝对金额从“工资”转移到“退出”/“退休”方面，即进入风险投资所称的“独角兽”状态时，问题就会出现。在某种程度上，赎金达到了勒索者一生中不再需要工作的地步，这些钱足以摆脱危险的行业并无限期地靠它生活。现在它不再是一个迭代的游戏，而是一个单一的游戏，并且在单一的游戏中，叛逃是可以得到奖励的。声誉是否被烧毁不再重要，相反，这可能是兑现所有累积声誉的时刻。

我认为总的来说，无论是光明面还是黑暗面，在给定的市场空间中这种“相变”都值得密切关注，因为它可能会导致“不知从何而来”的行为发生显着变化，并可能很快朝着丑陋的方向发展。

>Most hackers actually keep their promises if paid the ransom, nowadays.

I don't think that's actually true, or at least is certainly cannot be taken for granted. Instead, it appears ransom has followed more of the path of Silicon Valley VCs:

.It sounds perverse but the incentives require it: if payment didn't bring resolution, no one would pay. As a result, all of the big gangs avoid scamming.

What you're describing is the expected Game Theory outcome over long periods in an iterated game. This works as long as the payment amount is towards the <salary> side of the potential payment spectrum, where each payment may well be decent money for the work the ransomers put in but not so much that they don't need new ransoms. The problem comes if/when the absolute amount of payment moves from "salary" to the "Exit"/"Retirement" side of the spectrum, ie, heads into what VC would call "Unicorn" status. At some level of money it reaches the point where the ransomers need never work again in their lives, it's enough money to get out of the risky business and live off of it indefinitely. It's now no longer an iterated game but a single game, and in single games defection can be rewarded. It no longer matters if reputation is burned, on the contrary it might be the moment to cash all accumulated rep in.

I think in general, both on the bright and dark sides, this sort of "phase change" in a given market space is worth trying to keep an eye out for because it can result in significantly changed behavior "out of nowhere" that can head in ugly directions very fast.

在 HN 中查看
nicce 2026/04/12 02:16
是的，这项业务是基于实际兑现承诺的。
Yeah, this business is based on actually delivering the promise.
在 HN 中查看
DANmode 2026/04/12 13:01
这就是人们付费的原因。

要点是：它还会将您标记为已知付款人，

重复命中。

That’s WHY people pay.

The point being made is: it also flags you as a known-payer,

for a repeat hit.

在 HN 中查看
2OEH8eoCRo0 2026/04/12 03:29
关键在于，通过付费来激励它并使其变得有价值，而不是黑客信守诺言。
The point is that by paying you incentivize it and make it worthwhile not that the hackers keep promises.
在 HN 中查看
chistev 2026/04/12 02:42
这是有道理的。那么他们应该付钱。
That makes sense. They should pay, then.
在 HN 中查看
nubg 2026/04/12 04:22
似乎这种商业模式有一个致命弱点：“好人”可以开始攻击公司，假装成团伙之一索要赎金，然后在支付赎金后故意继续攻击。正是为了摧毁这种商业模式。帮派会气愤，但他们却无能为力？除了试图追踪“好人”或引入某种（基于密码学或其他）证明系统来证明他们进行了黑客攻击之外？
Seems like there is an achilles heel for this business model: A "good guy" could start hacking companies, demand ransom while pretending to be one of the gangs, and then deliberately continuing the attack after the ransom is paid. Precisely to destroy this business model. The gangs would be fuming but there would be nothing they could do? Apart from trying to track down the "good guy" or introducing some sort of (cryptography based or whatever) proof-system that a hack was made by them?
在 HN 中查看
chistev 2026/04/12 04:51
这是一个有趣的想法。我正在等待看到对此的回应。
This is an interesting thought. I'm waiting to see responses to it.
在 HN 中查看
ndiddy 2026/04/12 05:20
> “永远不要支付赎金。拜托。即使您的企业会遭受损失，如果您支付赎金，也会遭受更多损失，因为现在知道您会屈服。另外：您正在让其他人的问题变得更大。”

如今，公司试图通过向“专门从事勒索软件恢复”并声称拥有“解密客户文件的专有商业秘密手段”的安全公司支付赎金来减轻与支付赎金相关的声誉损害。这些公司收取的费用总是高于其服务的赎金成本。然后，他们提供了一份未逐项列出的收据，双方都高兴地离开，无需承认任何事情。如果您有兴趣，这里有一篇关于此实践的好文章。 https://features.propublica.org/ransomware/ransomware-attack...

> "NEVER EVER PAY RANSOM MONEY. Please. Even if your business will suffer it will suffer a lot more if you do pay since now it is known you'll cave. Also: you are making the problem larger for others."

These days, companies try to mitigate the reputational harm associated with paying the ransom by instead paying security firms that "specialize in ransomware recovery" and claim to have "proprietary trade secret means of decrypting their clients' files". These firms always just happen to charge more than the cost of the ransom for their services. They then provide a non-itemized receipt, and both parties walk away happy and without having to admit to anything. Here's a good article on this practice if you're interested. https://features.propublica.org/ransomware/ransomware-attack...

在 HN 中查看
apt-apt-apt-apt 2026/04/12 06:49
老实说，现在对电子犯罪分子来说就像圣诞节一样。

如此多的新玩具和欺骗或勒索人们的方法。还有很多潜在的创新值得探索，哈哈。

Honestly, it's gotta feel like Christmas to e-criminals right now.

So many new toys and ways to scam or extort people. And so many potential innovations to explore as well lol.

gaythread 2026/04/12 00:56

在 HN 中查看
bakugo 2026/04/12 01:12
是的，但是 GTA5 在发布十年后就被泄露了。 Rockstar 并没有因此受到任何重大损害。

不过，如果《6》在发布前就泄露了，那就完全是另外一回事了。我可以想象如果发生这种情况他们实际上会支付赎金。

Yes, but GTA5 leaked a decade after its release. Rockstar didn't really suffer any significant damage from it.

If 6 leaks before release, though, that's a completely different story. I can imagine them actually paying a ransom if that happened.

在 HN 中查看
dvratil 2026/04/12 01:59
也许我漏掉了一些东西，但 GTA6 源代码泄露会对 Rockstar 造成怎样的伤害呢？我的意思是，不太可能从泄漏中编译出一个完整的工作游戏，即使是这样，这也是一项不平凡的任务，我不认为这会对销售造成太大影响。

我唯一能想象的是这个故事会在互联网上被破坏，但仅此而已。

Maybe I'm missing something, but how would GTA6 source leak really harm Rockstar? I mean it's unlikely it would be possible to compile a full working game from the leak, and even if so, it's such a non-trivial task, that I don't believe it would hurt sales /that/ much.

The only thing I can imagine is the story would get spoiled on the internet, but that's about it.

在 HN 中查看
JCattheATM 2026/04/12 11:25
人们会编译它，非官方的社区服务器很快就会出现。
People would compile it and unofficial community servers would quickly pop up.
在 HN 中查看
mh- 2026/04/12 02:28
我觉得有必要说一下 不应该是这样的, to avoid an onslaught of replies, but:

发现和利用多人游戏体验中的漏洞/故障会变得更加容易，这是他们的摇钱树。

I feel the need to say it shouldn't be this way, to avoid an onslaught of replies, but:

It would be dramatically easier to discover and exploit vulnerabilities/glitches in their multiplayer experience, which is their cash cow.

3eb7988a1663 2026/04/12 03:02

在 HN 中查看
mgol94 2026/04/12 02:38
我推测这并不是个人无需付费即可编译和玩的问题，而是通过访问代码库，创建破解和在线作弊将是微不足道的，这实际上可能会损害他们的底线
I would speculate that it’s not about individuals compiling and playing without paying, but that with access to the codebase, creating cracks and online cheats would be trivial, which might actually hurt their bottom line
在 HN 中查看
esskay 2026/04/12 04:19
当他们的在线平台推出时，要阻止对其的滥用将是一件很痛苦的事情，考虑到《侠盗猎车手 5》在线模式赚了数十亿美元，这在财务上是有问题的。
It'd make it a pain to stop abuse of their online platform when it launches, which is financially problematic given gta 5 online made rockstar billions.
